introSection:
Scenic Railway is a wooden roller coaster located at Dreamland Margate in Margate, Kent, England, UK.

While this was the first roller coaster constructed at Dreamland, it is not the first to operate in Margate. The Switchback Railway operated from 1888 to 1897.

Originally opened in 1920, the Scenic Railway was rebuilt and reopened in 2015. It has not operated since an incident in 2024 where part of the track was damaged.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
On 21 August 1949, a third of the Scenic Railway was destroyed in a fire in the early morning.

On 7 April 2008, a large part of the Scenic Railway, including a shed housing the trains, was destroyed after it was set on fire deliberately.

Dreamland reopened on 19 June 2015, however, the reopening of the Scenic Railway was delayed. The ride reopened to the public on 16 October 2015 and to the press the previous day.

On 10 August 2024, a fault with the train led to the track being damaged as the train passed over it. In the affected area, the wood between the rails was broken. The train was stopped and riders evacuated.

On 27 January 2026, Dreamland Margate confirmed that Scenic Railway would be permanently closed. The park also stated that the ride would remain standing due to its Grade II* status with the proposal that it will be repurposed in a new way.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: The Scenic Railway has been painted white, blue and brown in the past. When it was rebuilt in 2015, it was left unpainted.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train is still operated by a brakeman, however when the trains were rebuilt a magnetic braking system was added which regulates their speed.

fullText:
Scenic Railway is a wooden roller coaster located at Dreamland Margate in Margate, Kent, England, UK.

While this was the first roller coaster constructed at Dreamland, it is not the first to operate in Margate. The Switchback Railway operated from 1888 to 1897.

Originally opened in 1920, the Scenic Railway was rebuilt and reopened in 2015. It has not operated since an incident in 2024 where part of the track was damaged.

On 21 August 1949, a third of the Scenic Railway was destroyed in a fire in the early morning.

On 7 April 2008, a large part of the Scenic Railway, including a shed housing the trains, was destroyed after it was set on fire deliberately.

Dreamland reopened on 19 June 2015, however, the reopening of the Scenic Railway was delayed. The ride reopened to the public on 16 October 2015 and to the press the previous day.

On 10 August 2024, a fault with the train led to the track being damaged as the train passed over it. In the affected area, the wood between the rails was broken. The train was stopped and riders evacuated.

On 27 January 2026, Dreamland Margate confirmed that Scenic Railway would be permanently closed. The park also stated that the ride would remain standing due to its Grade II* status with the proposal that it will be repurposed in a new way.



The Scenic Railway has been painted white, blue and brown in the past. When it was rebuilt in 2015, it was left unpainted.

The train is still operated by a brakeman, however when the trains were rebuilt a magnetic braking system was added which regulates their speed.
